# Cat Ski 1989

> A free browser game inspired by SkiFree (1991). Play as a cat skiing down an endless mountain. Three regional themes, mid-air tricks, dog and snowman chasers, tunable difficulty. Single self-contained HTML file with no build step or install required.

Cat Ski 1989 is an open-source browser game that recreates the SkiFree (Windows 3.0, 1991) gameplay loop with a cat as the player character. It includes three regional mountain themes (Alps, Rockies, Himalayas), four cat color variants, an in-air trick system with chained scoring, gold escape ramps that spawn alongside each dog wave, and a snowman that takes the role of the original yeti. Difficulty is fully tunable through two sliders (intensity and dog count) with four named presets. The game supports keyboard and touch input, has Web Audio synthesized sound effects, bilingual flavor text (English and rioplatense Spanish), per-difficulty best score tracking, and a 9-lives casual mode. The entire game is approximately 150 KB of vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Installable as a Progressive Web App for offline play. Hosted on Vercel, source on GitHub.

## Pages

- [Play Cat Ski](https://ski.mike-lee.me/): The game itself. Loads in a browser and is playable on desktop or mobile.
- [How to Use Claude to Build a Browser Game](https://ski.mike-lee.me/readme.html): A walkthrough article covering the project's stack, per-element design criteria, the workflow used to build it with Claude as a design collaborator, the photography-derived color palette approach for the three mountain themes, and a free toolchain readers can use to build a similar project.

## About the project

- Author: Mike Lee (Whisker Leaks)
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mikelee89/
- Source code: https://github.com/ashtonmorrow/cat-ski
- License: open source (see repository)
- Inspiration: SkiFree (1991, Microsoft Entertainment Pack 3 for Windows 3.0)
- Stack: vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; HTML5 Canvas; Web Audio API; no build step; no framework; no runtime dependencies beyond Google Fonts
- Hosting: Vercel, deployed automatically from the GitHub repository
- PWA: installable on iOS, Android, and desktop via the in-app INSTALL button

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ashtonmorrow/cat-ski): Issues, forks, and pull requests welcome.
- [Mike Lee on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mikelee89/): Project author contact.
